questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=\frac{\pi}{4}+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}\right)-\tan ^{-1} x$ and $a_i\left(a_i < a_{i+1} \forall i=1,2,3, \ldots \ldots, n\right)$ be the positive integral values of $x$ for which $\operatorname{sgn}(f(x))=1$ where $\operatorname{sgn}(\cdot)$ denotes signum function.Question: If $P(x)=x^2-4 k x+3 k^2$ is negative for all values of $x$ lying in the interval $\left(a_1, a_2\right)$ then set of real values of $k$ is:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{\pi}{4}+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}\right)-\tan ^{-1} x \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=\frac{\pi}{4}+\cot ^{-1} x-\tan ^{-1} x \\ & \quad=\frac{3 \pi}{4}-2 \tan ^{-1} x \quad\{\because x>0\} \\ & \operatorname{sgn}(f(x))=1 \Rightarrow f(x)>0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{3 \pi}{4}-2 \tan ^{-1} x>0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \tan ^{-1} x < \frac{3 \pi}{8} \Rightarrow x < \sqrt{2}+1\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad$ Possible positive integral values of $x$ are 1,2
$$
a_1=1 \text { and } a_2=2
$$
(ii)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& P(x)=x^2-4 k x+3 k^2 \\
& P(x) < 0 \quad \forall x \in\left(a_1, a_2\right) \\
& P(1) \leq 0 \quad \text { and } \quad P(2) \leq 0 \\
& 1-4 k+3 k^2 \leq 0 \text { and } 4-8 k+3 k^2 \leq 0
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
(3 k-1)(k-1) \leq 0 \quad(3 k-2)(k-2) \leq 0
$$
$k \in\left[\frac{1}{3}, 1\right]$
... (1) $k \in\left[\frac{2}{3}, 2\right]$
$\therefore \quad$ Intersection of eqns. (1) and (2) is $k \in\left[\frac{2}{3}, 2\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)=\frac{\pi}{4}+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}\right)-\tan ^{-1} x$ and $a_i\left(a_i < a_{i+1} \forall i=1,2,3, \ldots \ldots, n\right)$ be the positive integral values of $x$ for which $\operatorname{sgn}(f(x))=1$ where $\operatorname{sgn}(\cdot)$ denotes signum function.Question: The value of $\sum_{i=1}^n a_i$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\frac{\pi}{4}+\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{\sqrt{1+x^2}}\right)-\tan ^{-1} x \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=\frac{\pi}{4}+\cot ^{-1} x-\tan ^{-1} x \\ & \quad=\frac{3 \pi}{4}-2 \tan ^{-1} x \quad\{\because x>0\} \\ & \operatorname{sgn}(f(x))=1 \Rightarrow f(x)>0 \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{3 \pi}{4}-2 \tan ^{-1} x>0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \tan ^{-1} x < \frac{3 \pi}{8} \Rightarrow x < \sqrt{2}+1\end{aligned}$ $\therefore \quad$ Possible positive integral values of $x$ are 1,2 $$ a_1=1 \text { and } a_2=2 $$ (ii)
$$ \begin{aligned} & P(x)=x^2-4 k x+3 k^2 \\ & P(x) < 0 \quad \forall x \in\left(a_1, a_2\right) \\ & P(1) \leq 0 \quad \text { and } \quad P(2) \leq 0 \\ & 1-4 k+3 k^2 \leq 0 \text { and } 4-8 k+3 k^2 \leq 0 \end{aligned} $$ $$ (3 k-1)(k-1) \leq 0 \quad(3 k-2)(k-2) \leq 0 $$ $k \in\left[\frac{1}{3}, 1\right]$ ... (1) $k \in\left[\frac{2}{3}, 2\right] ... (2)$ $\therefore \quad$ Intersection of eqns. (1) and (2) is $k \in\left[\frac{2}{3}, 2\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
